channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: CNN Founder Ted Turner Dies at 87
item.description: 📺 US Politics - Ted Turner, the brash media pioneer who founded CNN and revolutionized 24-hour news broadcasting, has died at age 87. His vision transformed both the television industry and global news consumption.

item.title: Russia Cancels Military Hardware at Victory Day Parade for First Time in Decades
item.description: 🇷🇺 Ukraine-Russia War - For the first time in nearly 20 years, Russia will display no tanks or military vehicles at Moscow Victory Day parade - only soldiers marching. BBC Russia editor Steve Rosenberg says this signals the Ukraine war is not going to plan.

item.title: China Sentences Former Defense Ministers to Suspended Death
item.description: 🇨🇳 Asia-Pacific - China has announced suspended death sentences for former defense ministers Wei Fenghe and Li Shangfu following their ousting in a major military purge. The move highlights ongoing anti-corruption campaigns targeting top military brass.

item.title: Three IS-Linked Women Arrested in Australia After Syria Return
item.description: 🇦🇺 Middle East - Three women allegedly linked to Islamic State have been arrested in Australia after flying home from Syria for the first time in years. The arrests highlight ongoing concerns about returning foreign fighters.

item.title: Hungary Returns Seized $82 Million in Cash and Gold to Ukraine
item.description: 🇭🇺 Ukraine-Russia War - Hungary has returned a seized shipment worth $82 million in cash and gold to Ukraine, in a significant gesture after initially confiscating the assets. The move could signal improving Hungary-Ukraine relations.

item.title: Sweden's Gripen Fighter Deal With Ukraine Progressing Well
item.description: ⚔️ Ukraine-Russia War - Talks between Sweden and Ukraine for Gripen fighter jets are advancing positively, Swedish defense minister says. Ukraine continues seeking modern air capabilities.

item.title: Israel Kills Hezbollah Commander in First Beirut Strike Since Ceasefire
item.description: 💥 Middle East - Israel says it killed a Hezbollah commander in the first strike on Beirut since the ceasefire agreement, raising tensions along the Lebanon border.

item.title: Two Drones From Russia Crash in Latvia, Army Says
item.description: 🇱🇻 Europe - Two drones originating from Russia crashed in Latvia, the Latvian army reports. The incident adds to concerns about airspace violations and drone activity near NATO borders.

item.title: Rubio Meets Pope Leo as Trump Continues Attacks on Pontiff
item.description: ⛪ Diplomacy - Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Pope Leo amid ongoing tensions between the Trump administration and the Vatican. The Vatican stressed solid ties despite Trump's continued criticism.

item.title: 19 Kenyans Killed Fighting for Russia in Ukraine, Foreign Minister Says
item.description: 🇰🇪 Ukraine-Russia War - Kenya's foreign minister confirmed that 19 Kenyan nationals have been killed while fighting for Russia in Ukraine, highlighting the global reach of the conflict.

item.title: US Sanctions Iraq's Deputy Oil Minister for Helping Iran
item.description: 🇮🇶 Middle East - The US Treasury sanctioned Iraq's deputy oil minister for allegedly diverting oil revenues to Iran-backed groups. Washington intensifies pressure on Tehran's regional network.

item.title: Thailand and Cambodia Pledge Peace After Border Clashes
item.description: 🇹🇭 Asia-Pacific - Thailand and Cambodia pledged to forge lasting peace at an ASEAN meeting following recent border clashes. The two nations seek to prevent further escalation.

item.title: China Condemns US Raid on Venezuela, Seizure of Maduro
item.description: 🇨🇳 US-China - China reiterated its opposition to what it called the "hegemonic" US raid on Venezuela and seizure of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, condemning Washington's actions.

item.title: Kremlin Tightens Putin's Security Ahead of Victory Day Events
item.description: 🇷🇺 Ukraine-Russia War - The Kremlin announced tightened security for President Vladimir Putin ahead of Victory Day events, as Russia prepares for its annual military parade amid the ongoing war in Ukraine.

item.title: Taiwan Conducts First Torpedo Test Fire From Domestically-Made Submarine
item.description: 🇹🇼 Defense Industry - Taiwan successfully tested torpedoes from its domestically-built submarine, marking a major milestone in the island's defense capabilities amid rising tensions with China.

item.title: Ukraine's Top Negotiator in Miami Talks, Source Says
item.description: 🇺🇦 Ukraine-Russia War - Ukraine's lead negotiator is in Miami for talks with US officials, a source says, as diplomatic efforts continue to find a path to end the conflict.

item.title: China Asks Banks to Pause Loans to US-Sanctioned Refiners
item.description: 🇨🇳 Economy - China's financial regulator has advised major lenders to suspend new loans to refineries sanctioned by the US over Iranian oil ties. Bloomberg reports the move shows Beijing responding to Washington pressure.

item.title: Trump Sees Swift End to Iran War as Tehran Reviews US Peace Proposal
item.description: 🇮🇷 US-Iran War - The US and Iran are edging toward a temporary agreement to halt their war, sources and officials say. Tehran is reviewing a proposal that would stop fighting but leave contentious issues unresolved.

item.title: US Submarines Rule Beneath the Waves, But China's Are Catching Up
item.description: 🌊 Military - The Economist analyzes how American submarines maintain underwater dominance but face growing competition from Chinese naval capabilities in the strategic undersea domain.

item.title: Pakistan-India War Anniversary Marked Amid Ongoing Tensions Over Kashmir
item.description: 🇵🇰 South Asia - AP News marks the anniversary of the Pakistan-India war with ongoing tensions over Kashmir remaining unresolved despite diplomatic efforts.

item.title: IOC Eases Belarus Restrictions, Russia Remains Banned
item.description: 🏅 Diplomacy - The International Olympic Committee moved to ease restrictions on Belarusian athletes while maintaining its suspension of Russia ahead of upcoming games.

item.title: Most Hungarians Oppose Aid to Ukraine, Poll Shows
item.description: 🇭🇺 Europe - A poll shows most of Prime Minister Viktor Orban's voters oppose Hungarian aid to Ukraine, while Hungarians remain split on Kyiv's EU bid amid the ongoing war.

item.title: Israeli Arms Company Says Turkey Critic No Longer Works There
item.description: 🇹🇷 Middle East - An Israeli defense company stated that Shay Gal, a Turkish government critic who drew attention for his commentary, no longer works there. The statement came amid regional tensions.

item.title: Gulf States Derailed Trump's Project Freedom, Cutting US Airspace Access
item.description: 🇦🇪 Middle East - Gulf states have reportedly derailed Trump's "Project Freedom" initiative by cutting US access to critical airspace and bases, Middle East Eye reports.

item.title: China and US Drive Global Debt to Record $353 Trillion as Iran War Adds Pressure
item.description: 💰 Economy - A new report shows China and the US are driving global debt to a record $353 trillion, with the Iran war adding further economic pressure on nations worldwide.
